code_improvements.title: Initialize project scaffolding with standard Android/Kotlin structure
code_improvements.description: The repository has no application source code yet. Scaffold a standard Android project structure with Gradle build files, an Application class, a main Activity, and a manifest. This establishes the foundational code patterns that all future features will build upon and ensures the project can compile and run from the start.
code_improvements.rationale: Without a compilable project skeleton, no other code improvements or features can be developed or tested. This is the critical first step.

code_improvements.title: Add EditorConfig and code formatting configuration
code_improvements.description: Add an .editorconfig file and configure ktlint or detekt for consistent Kotlin code style across the project. Establishing formatting rules before any application code is written prevents style drift and reduces future reformatting churn.
code_improvements.rationale: Setting up code style enforcement early ensures all future contributions follow a consistent pattern from day one.

code_improvements.title: Configure CI pipeline with build and lint checks
code_improvements.description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the Gradle build, lint checks, and unit tests on every push and pull request. This provides immediate feedback on code quality and prevents broken code from being merged.
code_improvements.rationale: Early CI setup catches issues before they accumulate and establishes a quality-first development workflow.

code_improvements.title: Set up dependency injection framework
code_improvements.description: Integrate Hilt (or Koin) for dependency injection from the start. Structuring the app with DI from the beginning avoids costly refactoring later and makes the codebase inherently testable with clear separation of concerns.
code_improvements.rationale: Retrofitting dependency injection into an existing codebase is significantly harder than starting with it. This enables clean architecture patterns for the inventory management features.

code_improvements.title: Add Room database schema with inventory entity models
code_improvements.description: Define the core data layer using Room persistence library with entity classes for inventory items, categories, and locations. Include database migrations strategy and a repository pattern for data access.
code_improvements.rationale: An inventory management app is fundamentally data-driven. Establishing the data model and persistence layer early provides the foundation for all UI and business logic features.

metadata.reason: Project is in early initialization. Code improvement ideas focus on establishing foundational project structure, code quality tooling, and core architectural patterns needed for an Android inventory management application.
